description_long: This is a commercial passenger airplane with a streamlined fuselage painted in blue, green, and white, featuring the 'airflorida' branding along the side. The aircraft has a low-wing configuration with swept-back wings and two underwing turbofan engines. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with multiple windows visible for pilot visibility. The landing gear is in a tricycle arrangement, with the main gear under the wings and a nose gear at the front. The airplane's exterior is smooth and metallic, with visible passenger windows and a boarding stairway extended from the front left door.
description: A blue, green, and white commercial passenger airplane with swept low wings, twin underwing engines, and a conventional tail. Features include 'airflorida' branding, a forward cockpit, and a tricycle landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full fuselage, 'airflorida' branding, extended boarding stairway, and both wings with engines visible.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the nose, cockpit windows, wing sweep, and both engines under the wings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the tail logo, passenger windows, and the clean lines of the fuselage and wings.
description_view_3: Top-down view showing the full wingspan, engine placement, and the boarding stairway at the front left door.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, swept-back wings with slight dihedral and no visible winglets.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with nose and main gear units
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows
